message:
test(fcp-conformance/streaming-reconnect): pin ReconnectConfig backoff invariants + handler counter discipline (br-iyimk)

ReconnectConfig::delay_for_attempt is the documented backoff function
every streaming connector relies on. Its docstring (bead y54mi) names
THREE NORMATIVE invariants — floor (>= MIN_RECONNECT_DELAY unless
caller pins max_delay below it), ceiling (capped at max_delay), and
bounded jitter (uniform [0.8, 1.2] when enabled, tighter than legacy
±50%) — plus panic-safety guards for misconfigured multipliers and
huge max_delay. Zero conformance coverage despite the explicit
y54mi/upgdb pinning in source.

Adds 12 tests against the public fcp_streaming::{ReconnectConfig,
ReconnectHandler} API:

- default_config_returns_floored_delay_at_attempt_zero
- zero_initial_delay_is_floored_to_min_reconnect_delay (the 100 ms
  floor — protects against accidental hot-loop retry)
- max_delay_below_floor_overrides_floor (explicit user cap wins)
- delay_is_capped_at_max_delay (ceiling under aggressive multiplier)
- jitter_stays_within_plus_minus_20_percent_band (256 samples, all
  must be in band)
- nan_backoff_multiplier_does_not_panic_and_falls_back_to_floor
- negative_backoff_multiplier_does_not_panic
- extreme_backoff_does_not_panic_at_high_attempt_count (10× × 1000
  attempts: f64::powi -> +inf path)
- huge_max_delay_does_not_panic (max_delay = Duration::MAX, the
  documented from_secs_f64 panic guard)
- record_failure_saturates_at_u32_max (counter integrity — can't
  reset retry budget by overflowing)
- record_failure_then_reset_clears_attempts_to_zero
- unlimited_attempts_can_reconnect_remains_true (max_attempts=None
  always permits)

All 12 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
streaming_reconnect_config_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
